title: scurrula lobeliiflora
intro: Scurrula lobeliiflora, also known as the poison tree or poison shrub, is a plant species found in tropical and subtropical regions, particularly in Asia and Australia. The plant's bark and leaves contain toxic compounds, including saponins and glycosides, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. Studies have shown that Scurrula lobeliiflora has potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as antimicrobial and antifungal properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, although it should be handled with caution due to its toxic compounds and potential side effects.
